english.text: If a monk, overcome by lust and with a distorted mind, speaks indecent words to a woman, as a young man might to a young woman and referring to sexual intercourse, he commits an offense entailing suspension.

english.author: sujato
english.text: But suppose a monk overwhelmed with lust talks dirty to a female in a lewd way, like a boy with a girl.  This results in suspension.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Suspension 4: encouraging a woman to satisfy one’s desires

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Suspension 4: Encouraging Someone to Serve One’s Desires

english.author: sujato
english.text: 4. Serving His Desires

segments.pali: Yo pana bhikkhu otiṇṇo vipariṇatena cittena mātugāmassa santike attakāmapāricariyāya vaṇṇaṁ bhāseyya “etadaggaṁ bhagini pāricariyānaṁ yā mādisaṁ sīlavantaṁ kalyāṇadhammaṁ brahmacāriṁ etena dhammena paricareyyā”ti methunupasaṁhitena, saṅghādiseso.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk, overcome by lust and with a distorted mind, encourages a woman to satisfy his own desires, saying, ‘Sister, she provides the highest service who in this way satisfies one like me, who is virtuous, celibate, and of good character,’ and if it is a reference to sexual intercourse, he commits an offense entailing suspension.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk, overcome by lust and with a distorted mind, encourages a woman to satisfy his own desires, saying, ‘Sister, she provides the highest service who in this way satisfies one like me, who is virtuous, celibate, and of good character,’ and if it is a reference to sexual intercourse, he commits an offense entailing suspension.

english.author: sujato
english.text: But suppose a monk overwhelmed with lust speaks to a female in praise of serving his own desires. Referring to sexual intercourse, he says, ‘Sister, the highest service is to perform such a service for one like me, who is virtuous, chaste, and of good character.’ This results in suspension.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Suspension 5: matchmaking

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Suspension 5: Matchmaking

english.author: sujato
english.text: 5. Acting as a Matchmaker

segments.pali: Yo pana bhikkhu sañcarittaṁ samāpajjeyya itthiyā vā purisamatiṁ purisassa vā itthimatiṁ, jāyattane vā jārattane vā, antamaso taṅkhaṇikāyapi, saṅghādiseso.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk acts as a matchmaker, conveying a man’s intention to a woman or a woman’s intention to a man, for marriage or for an affair, even if just a brief one, he commits an offense entailing suspension.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk acts as a matchmaker, conveying a man’s intention to a woman or a woman’s intention to a man, for marriage or for an affair, even if just a brief one, he commits an offense entailing suspension.

english.author: sujato
english.text: But suppose a monk acts as a matchmaker, communicating the intentions of a man to a woman, or a woman to a man. Regardless of whether it is for marriage or for an affair, or even just for a brief encounter, it results in suspension.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Suspension 6: building a hut inappropriately

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Suspension 6: Building a Hut

english.author: sujato
english.text: 6. Building a Hut

segments.pali: Saññācikāya pana bhikkhunā kuṭiṁ kārayamānena assāmikaṁ attuddesaṁ pamāṇikā kāretabbā, tatridaṁ pamāṇaṁ, dīghaso dvādasa vidatthiyo sugatavidatthiyā, tiriyaṁ sattantarā,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When a monk, by means of begging, builds a hut without a sponsoring owner and intended for himself, it is to be no more than twelve standard handspans long and seven wide inside.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: When a monk, by means of begging, builds a hut without a sponsoring owner and intended for himself, it is to be no more than twelve standard handspans long and seven wide inside.

english.author: sujato
english.text: But suppose a monk is building a hut for his own use using materials he has begged himself, without a sponsor. He must build it within the specified measurements: twelve spans long and seven spans wide.

segments.pali: bhikkhū abhinetabbā vatthudesanāya, tehi bhikkhūhi vatthu desetabbaṁ anārambhaṁ saparikkamanaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: He must have monks approve a site where no harm will be done and which has space on all sides.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: He must have monks approve a site where no harm will be done and which has space on all sides.

english.author: sujato
english.text: He must bring monks to appoint the site. Those monks must appoint a site where no harm will be done and which has sufficient space on all sides.

segments.pali: Sārambhe ce bhikkhu vatthusmiṁ aparikkamane saññācikāya kuṭiṁ kāreyya, bhikkhū vā anabhineyya vatthudesanāya, pamāṇaṁ vā atikkāmeyya, saṅghādiseso.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk, by means of begging, builds a hut on a site where harm will be done and which lacks space on all sides, or he does not have monks approve the site, or he exceeds the right size, he commits an offense entailing suspension.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk, by means of begging, builds a hut on a site where harm will be done and which lacks space on all sides, or he does not have monks approve the site, or he exceeds the right size, he commits an offense entailing suspension.

english.author: sujato
english.text: If a monk builds a hut using materials he has begged himself on a site where harm will be done and which lacks sufficient space on all sides, or he does not bring monks to appoint the site, or he exceeds the specified measurements, this results in suspension.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Suspension 7: building a dwelling inappropriately

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Suspension 7: Building a Dwelling

english.author: sujato
english.text: 7. Building a Large Dwelling

segments.pali: Mahallakaṁ pana bhikkhunā vihāraṁ kārayamānena sassāmikaṁ attuddesaṁ bhikkhū abhinetabbā vatthudesanāya,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When a monk builds a large dwelling with a sponsoring owner and intended for himself,

english.author: pasanna
english.text: When a monk builds a large dwelling with a sponsoring owner and intended for himself,

english.author: sujato
english.text: But suppose a monk is building a large dwelling for his own use with a sponsor. He must bring monks to appoint the site.

segments.pali: tehi bhikkhūhi vatthu desetabbaṁ anārambhaṁ saparikkamanaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: he must have monks approve a site where no harm will be done and which has space on all sides.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: he must have monks approve a site where no harm will be done and which has space on all sides.

english.author: sujato
english.text: They must appoint a site where no harm will be done and which has sufficient space on all sides.

segments.pali: Sārambhe ce bhikkhu vatthusmiṁ aparikkamane mahallakaṁ vihāraṁ kāreyya, bhikkhū vā anabhineyya vatthudesanāya, saṅghādiseso.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk builds a large dwelling on a site where harm will be done and which lacks space on all sides, or he does not have monks approve the site, he commits an offense entailing suspension.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk builds a large dwelling on a site where harm will be done and which lacks space on all sides, or he does not have monks approve the site, he commits an offense entailing suspension.

english.author: sujato
english.text: If a monk builds a large dwelling on a site where harm will be done and which lacks sufficient space on all sides, or he does not bring monks to appoint the site, this results in suspension.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Suspension 8: making a groundless accusation out of anger

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Suspension 8: Making a Groundless Accusation Out of Anger

english.author: sujato
english.text: 8. Angry and Upset

segments.pali: Yo pana bhikkhu bhikkhuṁ duṭṭho doso appatīto amūlakena pārājikena dhammena anuddhaṁseyya “appeva nāma naṁ imamhā brahmacariyā cāveyya”nti,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk who is angry and displeased groundlessly charges a monk with an offense entailing expulsion, aiming to make him leave the monastic life,

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk who is angry and displeased groundlessly charges a monk with an offense entailing expulsion, aiming to make him leave the monastic life,

english.author: sujato
english.text: But suppose a monk who is angry and upset groundlessly accuses another monk of an offense entailing expulsion, hoping to make him fall away from the monastic life.

segments.pali: tato aparena samayena samanuggāhīyamāno vā asamanuggāhīyamāno vā amūlakañceva taṁ adhikaraṇaṁ hoti, bhikkhu ca dosaṁ patiṭṭhāti, saṅghādiseso.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and then after some time, whether he is questioned or not, it is clear that the legal issue is groundless, and he admits to his ill will, he commits an offense entailing suspension.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: and then after some time, whether he is questioned or not, it is clear that the legal issue is groundless, and he admits to his ill will, he commits an offense entailing suspension.

english.author: sujato
english.text: Some time later, regardless of whether he is questioned or not, if it becomes clear that the legal issue is groundless, and that the monk acted out of anger, this results in suspension.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Suspension 9: making a groundless accusation out of anger, using a pretext

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Suspension 9: Making an Accusation Out of Anger on an Irrelevant Pretext

english.author: sujato
english.text: 9. Irrelevant Pretext

segments.pali: Yo pana bhikkhu bhikkhuṁ duṭṭho doso appatīto aññabhāgiyassa adhikaraṇassa kiñcidesaṁ lesamattaṁ upādāya pārājikena dhammena anuddhaṁseyya “appeva nāma naṁ imamhā brahmacariyā cāveyya”nti,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk who is angry and displeased, uses an unrelated legal issue as a pretext to charge a monk with an offense entailing expulsion, aiming to make him leave the monastic life,

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk who is angry and displeased, uses an unrelated legal issue as a pretext to charge a monk with an offense entailing expulsion, aiming to make him leave the monastic life,’

english.author: sujato
english.text: But suppose a monk who is angry and upset uses some irrelevant pretext to accuse another monk of an offense entailing expulsion, hoping to make him fall away from the monastic life.

segments.pali: tato aparena samayena samanuggāhīyamāno vā asamanuggāhīyamāno vā aññabhāgiyañceva taṁ adhikaraṇaṁ hoti kocideso lesamatto upādinno, bhikkhu ca dosaṁ patiṭṭhāti, saṅghādiseso.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and then after some time, whether he is questioned or not, it is clear that the legal issue is unrelated and was used as a pretext, and he admits to his ill will, he commits an offense entailing suspension.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: and then after some time, whether he is questioned or not, it is clear that the legal issue is unrelated and was used as a pretext, and he admits to his ill will, he commits an offense entailing suspension.

english.author: sujato
english.text: Some time later, regardless of whether he is questioned or not, if it becomes clear that the legal issue is irrelevant, and that the monk acted out of anger, this results in suspension.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Suspension 10: causing a schism in the Sangha

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Suspension 10: Causing Schism in the Saṅgha

english.author: sujato
english.text: 10. Schism in the Saṅgha

segments.pali: Yo pana bhikkhu samaggassa saṅghassa bhedāya parakkameyya, bhedanasaṁvattanikaṁ vā adhikaraṇaṁ samādāya paggayha tiṭṭheyya, so bhikkhu bhikkhūhi evamassa vacanīyo

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk pursues schism in a united Sangha or persists in taking up a legal issue conducive to schism, the monks should correct him like this:

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk pursues schism in a united Sangha or persists in taking up a legal issue conducive to schism, the monks should correct him like this,

english.author: sujato
english.text: But suppose a monk agitates for a schism in a united Saṅgha or persists in taking up a legal issue leading to schism. The monks should say to him,

segments.pali: “māyasmā samaggassa saṅghassa bhedāya parakkami, bhedanasaṁvattanikaṁ vā adhikaraṇaṁ samādāya paggayha aṭṭhāsi,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘Venerable, don’t pursue schism in the united Sangha or persist in taking up a legal issue conducive to schism.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: ‘Venerable, don’t pursue schism in the united Sangha or persist in taking up a legal issue conducive to schism.

english.author: sujato
english.text: ‘Venerable, don’t agitate for a schism in the unanimous Order or persist in taking up a legal issue leading to schism.

segments.pali: sametāyasmā saṅghena, samaggo hi saṅgho sammodamāno avivadamāno ekuddeso phāsu viharatī”ti,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Stay with the Sangha, for a united Sangha—in concord, in harmony, having a joint recitation—is at ease.’

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Stay with the Sangha, for a united Sangha—in concord, in harmony, having a joint recitation—is at ease.’

english.author: sujato
english.text: Join with the Order, for a unanimous Order—in concord, in harmony, reciting as one—lives at ease.’

segments.pali: evañca so bhikkhu bhikkhūhi vuccamāno tatheva paggaṇheyya, so bhikkhu bhikkhūhi yāvatatiyaṁ samanubhāsitabbo tassa paṭinissaggāya,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If that monk continues as before, the monks should press him up to three times to make him stop.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If that monk continues as before, the monks should press him up to three times to make him stop.

english.author: sujato
english.text: If that monk still continues as before, the monks should press him up to three times to make him stop.

segments.pali: yāvatatiyañce samanubhāsiyamāno taṁ paṭinissajjeyya, iccetaṁ kusalaṁ,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If he then stops, all is well.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If he then stops, all is well.

english.author: sujato
english.text: If he then stops, all is well.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If he does not stop, he commits an offense entailing suspension.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: he commits an offense entailing suspension.

english.author: sujato
english.text: If not, this results in suspension.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Suspension 11: siding with a schismatic

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Suspension 11: Following a Schismatic

english.author: sujato
english.text: 11. Following a Schismatic

segments.pali: Tasseva kho pana bhikkhussa bhikkhū honti anuvattakā vaggavādakā eko vā dve vā tayo vā, te evaṁ vadeyyuṁ

english.author: brahmali
english.text: That monk may have one, two, or three monks who side with him and support him, and they may say,

english.author: pasanna
english.text: That monk may have one, two, or three monks who side with him and support him, and they may say,

english.author: sujato
english.text: But that monk may have one, two, or three monks who are his followers and speak on behalf of his faction. They may say,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘Venerables, don’t correct this monk.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: “Venerables, don’t correct this monk.

english.author: sujato
english.text: ‘Venerables, don’t correct this monk.

segments.pali: dhammavādī ceso bhikkhu, vinayavādī ceso bhikkhu,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: He speaks in accordance with the Teaching and the training.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: He speaks in accordance with the Teaching and the training.

english.author: sujato
english.text: He speaks in accordance with the Teaching and the Training.

segments.pali: amhākañceso bhikkhu chandañca ruciñca ādāya voharati, jānāti, no bhāsati, amhākampetaṁ khamatī”ti,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: And he speaks with our consent and approval. He knows about us and speaks for us, and we approve of this.’

english.author: pasanna
english.text: And he speaks with our consent and approval. He knows about us and speaks for us, and we approve of this.”

english.author: sujato
english.text: And he conveys our wish and approval. He knows us and speaks for us, and we accept this.’

english.author: pasanna
english.text: The monks should correct those monks like this:

english.author: sujato
english.text: The monks should say to those monks,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘No, venerables,

english.author: pasanna
english.text: ‘No, Venerables,

english.author: sujato

english.author: pasanna

english.author: sujato

segments.pali: sametāyasmantānaṁ saṅghena, samaggo hi saṅgho sammodamāno avivadamāno ekuddeso phāsu viharatī”ti,

english.author: pasanna

english.author: sujato

english.author: pasanna

english.text: Suspension 12: being difficult to correct

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Suspension 12: Difficult to Correct

english.author: sujato
english.text: 12. Incorrigible

segments.pali: Bhikkhu paneva dubbacajātiko hoti uddesapariyāpannesu sikkhāpadesu bhikkhūhi sahadhammikaṁ vuccamāno attānaṁ avacanīyaṁ karoti

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk is difficult to correct, and he makes himself incorrigible when legitimately corrected by the monks concerning the training rules that are recited, saying,

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk is difficult to correct, and he makes himself incorrigible when legitimately corrected by the monks concerning the training rules that are recited, saying,

english.author: sujato
english.text: But then a monk is incorrigible. When he is justly admonished by the monks concerning the training rules that are recited, he closes himself off from admonishment, saying,

segments.pali: “mā maṁ āyasmanto kiñci avacuttha kalyāṇaṁ vā pāpakaṁ vā, ahampāyasmante na kiñci vakkhāmi kalyāṇaṁ vā pāpakaṁ vā,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘Venerables, don’t say anything to me, either good or bad, and I won’t say anything to you, either good or bad.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: “Venerables, don’t say anything to me, either good or bad, and I won’t say anything to you, either good or bad.

english.author: sujato
english.text: ‘Venerables, don’t say anything to me, either good or bad, and I won’t say anything to you, either good or bad.

english.author: brahmali

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Please refrain from correcting me,”

english.author: sujato

english.author: brahmali
english.text: then the monks should correct him like this:

english.author: pasanna
english.text: then the monks should correct him like this:

english.author: sujato
english.text: Then the monks should correct him in this way:

segments.pali: “māyasmā attānaṁ avacanīyaṁ akāsi, vacanīyamevāyasmā attānaṁ karotu,

english.author: brahmali

english.author: pasanna
english.text: ‘Be easy to correct, Venerable, not incorrigible.

english.author: sujato

english.author: pasanna
english.text: And please give legitimate correction to the monks, and the monks will do the same to you.

english.author: sujato

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Suspension 13: being a corrupter of families

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Suspension 13: Corrupters of Families

english.author: sujato
english.text: 13. A Corrupter of Families

english.author: pasanna

english.author: sujato
english.text: 1. Robe-making Season (1st)

segments.pali: Niṭṭhitacīvarasmiṁ bhikkhunā ubbhatasmiṁ kathine dasāhaparamaṁ atirekacīvaraṁ dhāretabbaṁ, taṁ atikkāmayato nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When his robe is finished and the robe season has ended, a monk should keep an extra robe for ten days at the most. If he keeps it longer than that, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: When his robe is finished and the robe season has ended, a monk should keep an extra robe for ten days at the most. If he keeps it longer than that, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

english.author: sujato
english.text: Extra robe-cloth may be kept by a monk for ten days at the most, so long as his robe is finished and the robe-making season has ended. If he keeps it beyond that, it entails relinquishment and confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Relinquishment and confession 2: staying apart from one’s robes

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Relinquishment With Confession 2: Staying Apart From Robes

english.author: sujato
english.text: 2. Robe-making Season (2nd)

segments.pali: Niṭṭhitacīvarasmiṁ bhikkhunā ubbhatasmiṁ kathine ekarattampi ce bhikkhu ticīvarena vippavaseyya, aññatra bhikkhusammutiyā nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When his robe is finished and the robe season has ended, if a monk stays apart from his three robes even for a single day, except if the monks have agreed, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: When his robe is finished and the robe season has ended, if a monk stays apart from his three robes even for a single day, except if the monks have agreed, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

english.author: sujato
english.text: A monk should not stay apart from his three robes even for a single day, so long as his robe is finished and the robe-making season has ended. This entails relinquishment and confession, unless it has been agreed on by the monks.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When his robe is finished and the robe season has ended, if out-of-season robe-cloth is offered to a monk, he may receive it if he wishes.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: When his robe is finished and the robe season has ended, if out-of-season robe-cloth is given to a monk, he may receive it if he wishes.

english.author: sujato
english.text: Out-of-season robe-cloth may be received by a monk if he so wishes, so long as his robe is finished and the robe-making season has ended.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If he receives it, he should quickly make a robe.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If he receives it, he should quickly make a robe.

english.author: sujato
english.text: However, once he has received it, he should make a robe right away.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If there is not enough cloth, but he is expecting more, he should keep it at most one month to make up the lack.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If there is not enough cloth, but he is expecting more, he should keep it at most one month to make up the lack.

english.author: sujato
english.text: If there is not enough cloth, but he is expecting more, he may keep it at most one month to make up the lack.

segments.pali: Tato ce uttari nikkhipeyya satiyāpi paccāsāya, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: pasanna

english.author: brahmali

english.text: 5. Receiving a Robe

segments.pali: Yo pana bhikkhu aññātikāya bhikkhuniyā hatthato cīvaraṁ paṭiggaṇheyya aññatra pārivattakā, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: pasanna

english.author: sujato
english.text: But suppose a monk receives robe-cloth from the hand of an unrelated nun. This entails relinquishment and confession, unless it is in exchange.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk asks an unrelated male or female householder for a robe, except on an appropriate occasion, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

english.author: pasanna

english.author: sujato
english.text: But suppose a monk asks an unrelated layman or laywoman for robe-cloth. This entails relinquishment and confession, except for certain occasions.

segments.pali: Tatthāyaṁ samayo, acchinnacīvaro vā hoti bhikkhu, naṭṭhacīvaro vā, ayaṁ tattha samayo.

english.text: If an unrelated male or female householder invites that monk to take many robes, he should accept at most one sarong and one upper robe. If he accepts more than that, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

segments.pali: Navaṁ pana bhikkhunā santhataṁ kārayamānena dve bhāgā suddhakāḷakānaṁ eḷakalomānaṁ ādātabbā, tatiyaṁ odātānaṁ, catutthaṁ gocariyānaṁ.

english.text: If a monk is having a new blanket made, he should use two parts of entirely black wool, a third part of white, and a fourth part of brown.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk is having a new blanket made, he should use two parts of entirely black wool, a third part of white, and a fourth part of brown.

english.author: sujato
english.text: But when a new rug is being made by a monk, it should include two parts of pure black wool, a third of white, and a fourth of brown.

segments.pali: Anādā ce bhikkhu dve bhāge suddhakāḷakānaṁ eḷakalomānaṁ, tatiyaṁ odātānaṁ, catutthaṁ gocariyānaṁ, navaṁ santhataṁ kārāpeyya, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ.

english.text: But when a monk has had a new rug made, they should wear it for six years.

english.text: If they have another new rug made within six years, regardless of whether they have relinquished the old rug or not, this entails relinquishment and confession, unless it is agreed on by the monks.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Relinquishment and confession 15: making an inappropriate sitting blanket

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Relinquishment With Confession 15: A Sitting-Blanket

english.author: sujato
english.text: 15. A Sitting Rug

segments.pali: Nisīdanasanthataṁ pana bhikkhunā kārayamānena purāṇasanthatassa sāmantā sugatavidatthi ādātabbā dubbaṇṇakaraṇāya.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk is having a sitting blanket made, he must incorporate a piece of one standard handspan from the border of an old blanket in order to make it ugly.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk is having a sitting-blanket made, he must incorporate a piece of one standard handspan from the border of an old blanket in order to make it ugly.

english.author: sujato
english.text: But when a sitting rug is being made by a monk, it should include a border made of an old rug to the width of a sugata-span in order to disfigure it.

segments.pali: Anādā ce bhikkhu purāṇasantha tassa sāmantā sugatavidatthiṁ, navaṁ nisīdanasanthataṁ kārāpeyya, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If he has a new sitting blanket made without incorporating a piece of one standard handspan from the border of an old blanket, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If he has a new sitting-blanket made without incorporating a piece of one standard handspan from the border of an old blanket, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

english.author: sujato
english.text: If a monk has a new sitting rug made without including a border made of an old rug to the width of a sugata-span, this entails relinquishment and confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Relinquishment and confession 16: carrying wool too far

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Relinquishment With Confession 16: Carrying Wool

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If wool is given to a monk who is traveling, he may receive it if he wishes.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If wool is given to a monk who is traveling, he may receive it if he wishes.

segments.pali: ākaṅkhamānena bhikkhunā paṭiggahetabbāni, paṭiggahetvā tiyojanaparamaṁ sahatthā haritabbāni asante hārake.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If he receives it and there is no-one else to carry it, he may carry it himself for at most 40 kilometers.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If he receives it and there is no one else to carry it, he may carry it himself for at most 40 kilometers.

segments.pali: Tato ce uttari hareyya, asantepi hārake, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If he carries it further than that, even if there is no-one else to carry it, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If he carries it further than that, even if there is no one else to carry it, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Relinquishment and confession 17: getting a nun to wash wool

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Relinquishment With Confession 17: Getting a Nun to Wash Wool

segments.pali: Yo pana bhikkhu aññātikāya bhikkhuniyā eḷakalomāni dhovāpeyya vā rajāpeyya vā vijaṭāpeyya vā, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk has an unrelated nun wash, dye, or comb wool, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk has an unrelated nun wash, dye, or comb wool, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Relinquishment and confession 18: becoming the owner of money

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Relinquishment With Confession 18: Money

segments.pali: Yo pana bhikkhu jātarūparajataṁ uggaṇheyya vā uggaṇhāpeyya vā upanikkhittaṁ vā sādiyeyya, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk takes, has someone else take, or consents to gold, silver, or money being deposited for him, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk takes, has someone else take, or consents to gold and silver being deposited for him, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Relinquishment and confession 19: trading with money

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Relinquishment With Confession 19: Trading With Money

segments.pali: Yo pana bhikkhu nānappakārakaṁ rūpiyasaṁvohāraṁ samāpajjeyya, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk engages in various kinds of trades involving money, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk trades with money in various ways, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Relinquishment and confession 20: bartering

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Relinquishment With Confession 20: Bartering

segments.pali: Yo pana bhikkhu nānappakārakaṁ kayavikkayaṁ samāpajjeyya, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk engages in various kinds of barter, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk barters in various ways, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.’

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The second subchapter on silk is finished.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: The second sub-chapter on silk is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The subchapter on almsbowls

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Relinquishment and confession 21: keeping an extra almsbowl beyond ten days

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Relinquishment With Confession 21: An Extra Almsbowl

segments.pali: Dasāhaparamaṁ atirekapatto dhāretabbo, taṁ atikkāmayato nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A monk should keep an extra almsbowl for ten days at the most. If he keeps it longer than that, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: A monk should keep an extra almsbowl for ten days at the most. If he keeps it longer than that, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Relinquishment and confession 22: exchanging an almsbowl with fewer than five mends

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Relinquishment With Confession 22: An Almsbowl With Fewer Than Five Mends

segments.pali: Yo pana bhikkhu ūnapañcabandhanena pattena aññaṁ navaṁ pattaṁ cetāpeyya, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk exchanges an almsbowl with fewer than five mends for a new almsbowl, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk exchanges an almsbowl with fewer than five mends for a new almsbowl, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

segments.pali: Tena bhikkhunā so patto bhikkhuparisāya nissajjitabbo,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: That monk should relinquish that almsbowl to a gathering of monks.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: That monk should relinquish that almsbowl to a gathering of monks.

segments.pali: yo ca tassā bhikkhuparisāya pattapariyanto, so tassa bhikkhuno padātabbo “ayaṁ te bhikkhu patto yāva bhedanāya dhāretabbo”ti,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: He should then be given the last almsbowl belonging to that gathering: ‘Monk, this bowl is yours. Keep it until it breaks.’

english.author: pasanna
english.text: He should then be given the last almsbowl belonging to that gathering: ‘Monk, this bowl is yours. Keep it until it breaks.’

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This is the proper procedure.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: This is the proper procedure.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Relinquishment and confession 23: storing the five tonics too long

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Relinquishment With Confession 23: The Five Tonics

segments.pali: Yāni kho pana tāni gilānānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ paṭisāyanīyāni bhesajjāni, seyyathidaṁ—sappi navanītaṁ telaṁ madhu phāṇitaṁ, tāni paṭiggahetvā sattāhaparamaṁ sannidhikārakaṁ paribhuñjitabbāni,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: After being received, the tonics allowable for sick monks—that is, ghee, butter, oil, honey, and syrup—should be used from storage for at most seven days.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: After being received, the tonics allowable for sick monks—that is, ghee, butter, oil, honey, and syrup—should be used from storage for at most seven days.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If one uses them longer than that, one commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If one uses them longer than that, one commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Relinquishment and confession 24: making a rainy-season robe at the wrong time

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Relinquishment With Confession 24: Rainy-Season Robes

segments.pali: “Māso seso gimhāna”nti bhikkhunā vassikasāṭikacīvaraṁ pariyesitabbaṁ, “addhamāso seso gimhāna”nti katvā nivāsetabbaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When there is a month left of summer, a monk may go looking for cloth for his rainy-season robe. When there is a half-month left, he may sew it and then wear it.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: When there is a month left of the hot season, a monk may go looking for cloth for his rainy-season robe. When there is a half-month left, he may sew it and then wear it.

segments.pali: Orena ce “māso seso gimhāna”nti vassikasāṭikacīvaraṁ pariyeseyya, orena“ddhamāso seso gimhāna”nti katvā nivāseyya, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If he goes looking for cloth for his rainy-season robe when there is more than a month left of summer, or if he sews it and then wears it when there is more than a half-month left, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If he goes looking for cloth for his rainy-season robe when there is more than a month left of the hot season, or if he sews it and then wears it when there is more than a half-month left, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Relinquishment and confession 25: taking back robe-cloth in anger

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Relinquishment With Confession 25: Taking Back Robe-Cloth

segments.pali: Yo pana bhikkhu bhikkhussa sāmaṁ cīvaraṁ datvā kupito anattamano acchindeyya vā acchindāpeyya vā, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk himself gives a robe to a monk, but then, in anger, takes it back or has it taken back, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk himself gives a robe to a monk, but then, in anger, takes it back or has it taken back, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Relinquishment and confession 26: asking for thread

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Relinquishment With Confession 26: Asking for Thread

segments.pali: Yo pana bhikkhu sāmaṁ suttaṁ viññāpetvā tantavāyehi cīvaraṁ vāyāpeyya, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk himself asks for thread, and then has weavers weave him robe-cloth, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk himself asks for thread, and then has weavers weave him robe-cloth, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Relinquishment and confession 27: bribing weavers

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Relinquishment With Confession 27: Bribing Weavers

segments.pali: Bhikkhuṁ paneva uddissa aññātako gahapati vā gahapatānī vā tantavāyehi cīvaraṁ vāyāpeyya, tatra ce so bhikkhu pubbe appavārito tantavāye upasaṅkamitvā cīvare vikappaṁ āpajjeyya

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a male or female householder is having robe-cloth woven by weavers for an unrelated monk and, without first being invited, that monk goes to those weavers and specifies the kind of robe-cloth he wants, saying,

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a male or female householder is having robe-cloth woven by weavers for an unrelated monk and, without first being invited, that monk goes to those weavers and specifies the kind of robe-cloth he wants, saying,

segments.pali: “idaṁ kho, āvuso, cīvaraṁ maṁ uddissa viyyati, āyatañca karotha, vitthatañca, appitañca, suvītañca, suppavāyitañca, suvilekhitañca, suvitacchitañca karotha,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘This robe-cloth that you are weaving for me, make it long and wide; make it closely woven, well-woven, well-stretched, well-scraped, and well-combed,

english.author: pasanna
english.text: ‘This robe-cloth that you are weaving for me, make it long and wide; make it closely woven, well-woven, well-stretched, well-scraped, and well-combed,

segments.pali: appeva nāma mayampi āyasmantānaṁ kiñcimattaṁ anupadajjeyyāmā”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and perhaps I will even give you a small gift,’

english.author: pasanna
english.text: and perhaps I will even give you a small gift,’

segments.pali: Evañca so bhikkhu vatvā kiñcimattaṁ anupadajjeyya antamaso piṇḍapātamattampi, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: then, in saying that and afterwards giving them a small gift, even a bit of almsfood, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: then, in saying that and afterwards giving them a small gift, even a bit of almsfood, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Relinquishment and confession 28: storing haste-cloth too long

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Relinquishment With Confession 28: Haste-Cloth

segments.pali: Dasāhānāgataṁ kattikatemāsikapuṇṇamaṁ bhikkhuno paneva accekacīvaraṁ uppajjeyya, accekaṁ maññamānena bhikkhunā paṭiggahetabbaṁ, paṭiggahetvā yāva cīvarakālasamayaṁ nikkhipitabbaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When there are ten days left to the Kattika full moon that ends the first rainy-season residence and a haste-cloth is given to a monk, he may receive it if he regards it as urgent. He may then store it until the end of the robe season.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: When there are ten days left to the Kattika full moon that ends the first rainy-season residence and haste-cloth is given to a monk, he may receive it if he regards it as urgent. He may then store it until the end of the robe season.

segments.pali: Tato ce uttari nikkhipeyya, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If he stores it beyond that, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If he stores it beyond that, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Relinquishment and confession 29: storing a robe for more than six days in an inhabited area

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Relinquishment With Confession 29: Keeping Robes while Staying in Risky Regions

segments.pali: Upavassaṁ kho pana kattikapuṇṇamaṁ yāni kho pana tāni āraññakāni senāsanāni sāsaṅkasammatāni sappaṭibhayāni, tathārūpesu bhikkhu senāsanesu viharanto ākaṅkhamāno tiṇṇaṁ cīvarānaṁ aññataraṁ cīvaraṁ antaraghare nikkhipeyya, siyā ca tassa bhikkhuno kocideva paccayo tena cīvarena vippavāsāya,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There are wilderness dwellings that are considered risky and dangerous. After observing the Kattika full moon that ends the rainy season, a monk who is staying in such a dwelling may, if he so desires, store one of his three robes in an inhabited area so long as he has a reason for staying apart from that robe.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: There are wilderness dwellings that are considered risky and dangerous. After observing the Kattika full moon that ends the rainy season, a monk who is staying in such a dwelling may, if he so desires, store one of his three robes in an inhabited area so long as he has a reason for staying apart from that robe.

segments.pali: chārattaparamaṁ tena bhikkhunā tena cīvarena vippavasitabbaṁ. Tato ce uttari vippavaseyya aññatra bhikkhusammutiyā, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: He should stay apart from that robe for six days at the most. If he stays apart from it longer than that, except if the monks have agreed, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: He should stay apart from that robe for six days at the most. If he stays apart from it longer than that, except if the monks have agreed, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Relinquishment and confession 30: diverting an offering intended for the Sangha

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Relinquishment With Confession 30: Offerings Intended for the Saṅgha

segments.pali: Yo pana bhikkhu jānaṁ saṅghikaṁ lābhaṁ pariṇataṁ attano pariṇāmeyya, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk diverts to himself material support that he knows was intended for the Sangha, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk diverts to himself material support that he knows was intended for the Sangha, he commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The third subchapter on almsbowls is finished.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: The Chapter on Offenses Entailing Relinquishment is Finished.

segments.pali: Uddiṭṭhā kho āyasmanto tiṁsa nissaggiyā pācittiyā dhammā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Venerables, the thirty rules on relinquishment and confession have been recited.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Venerables, the thirty rules on relinquishment and confession have been recited.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: In regard to this I ask you, ‘Are you pure in this?’

english.author: pasanna
english.text: In regard to this I ask you, ‘Are you pure in this?’

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A second time I ask, ‘Are you pure in this?’

english.author: pasanna
english.text: A second time I ask, ‘Are you pure in this?’

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A third time I ask, ‘Are you pure in this?’

english.author: pasanna
english.text: A third time I ask, ‘Are you pure in this?’

english.author: brahmali
english.text: You are pure in this and therefore silent. I will remember it thus.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: You are pure in this and therefore silent. I will remember it thus.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The offenses entailing relinquishment and confession are finished.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: The offenses entailing relinquishment and confession are finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The rules on confession

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Recitation of the Rules of Confession

segments.pali: Ime kho panāyasmanto dvenavuti pācittiyā dhammā uddesaṁ āgacchanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Venerables, these ninety-two rules on confession come up for recitation.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Venerables, these ninety-two rules on confession come up for recitation.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The subchapter on lying

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 1: lying

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 1: Lying

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk lies in full awareness, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk lies in full awareness, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 2: abusive speech

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 2: Abusive Speech

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk speaks abusively, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk speaks abusively, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 3: malicious talebearing

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 3: Malicious Talebearing

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk engages in malicious talebearing between monks, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk engages in malicious talebearing between monks, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 4: instructing a householder to memorize the Teaching

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 4: Memorizing the Teaching

english.author: sujato
english.text: 4. Teaching Dhamma Line by Line

segments.pali: Yo pana bhikkhu anupasampannaṁ padaso dhammaṁ vāceyya, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk instructs a person who is not fully ordained to memorize the Teaching, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk instructs a person who is not fully ordained to memorize the Teaching, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: sujato
english.text: But suppose a monk gets an unordained person to repeat the teaching line by line, this entails confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 5: lying down with one not fully ordained

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 5: Lying Down With One Not Fully Ordained

segments.pali: Yo pana bhikkhu anupasampannena uttaridirattatirattaṁ sahaseyyaṁ kappeyya, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk lies down more than two or three nights in the same sleeping place as a person who is not fully ordained, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk lies down more than two or three nights in the same sleeping place as a person who is not fully ordained, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 6: lying down with a woman

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 6: Lying Down With a Woman

segments.pali: Yo pana bhikkhu mātugāmena sahaseyyaṁ kappeyya, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk lies down in the same sleeping place as a woman, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk lies down in the same sleeping place as a woman, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 7: teaching a woman alone

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 7: Teaching Someone of the Opposite Gender Alone

segments.pali: Yo pana bhikkhu mātugāmassa uttarichappañcavācāhi dhammaṁ deseyya aññatra viññunā purisaviggahena, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk gives a teaching of more than five or six sentences to a woman, except in the presence of a man who understands, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk gives a teaching of more than five or six sentences to a woman, except in the presence of a man who understands, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 8: truthfully announcing a superhuman quality

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 8: Truthfully Announcing Superhuman Attainments

segments.pali: Yo pana bhikkhu anupasampannassa uttarimanussadhammaṁ āroceyya, bhūtasmiṁ pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk truthfully tells a person who is not fully ordained of a superhuman quality, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk truthfully tells a person who is not fully ordained of a superhuman quality, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 9: announcing a monk’s grave offense

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 9: Announcing a Grave Offense

segments.pali: Yo pana bhikkhu bhikkhussa duṭṭhullaṁ āpattiṁ anupasampannassa āroceyya aññatra bhikkhusammutiyā, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk tells a person who is not fully ordained about a monk’s grave offense, except if the monks have agreed, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk tells a person who is not fully ordained about a monk’s grave offense, except if the monks have agreed, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk digs the earth or has it dug, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk digs the earth or has it dug, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk destroys plants, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk speaks evasively or harasses, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 13: Complaining

english.author: pasanna

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 14: leaving furniture outside

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 14: Leaving Furniture Outside

segments.pali: Yo pana bhikkhu saṅghikaṁ mañcaṁ vā pīṭhaṁ vā bhisiṁ vā kocchaṁ vā ajjhokāse santharitvā vā santharāpetvā vā taṁ pakkamanto neva uddhareyya, na uddharāpeyya, anāpucchaṁ vā gaccheyya, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk takes a bed, a bench, a mattress, or a stool belonging to the Sangha and puts it out in the open or has it put out in the open, and he then departs without putting it away, having it put away, or informing anyone, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk takes a bed, a bench, a mattress, or a stool belonging to the Sangha and puts it outside or has it put outside, and he then departs without putting it away, having it put away, or informing anyone, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 15: not putting away bedding

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 15: Leaving Bedding Outside

segments.pali: Yo pana bhikkhu saṅghike vihāre seyyaṁ santharitvā vā santharāpetvā vā taṁ pakkamanto neva uddhareyya, na uddharāpeyya, anāpucchaṁ vā gaccheyya, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk puts out bedding in a dwelling belonging to the Sangha, or has it put out, and he then departs without putting it away, having it put away, or informing anyone, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk puts out bedding in a dwelling belonging to the Sangha, or has it put out, and he then departs without putting it away, having it put away, or informing anyone, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 16: encroaching on a monk

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 16: Encroaching on a Monastic

segments.pali: Yo pana bhikkhu saṅghike vihāre jānaṁ pubbupagataṁ bhikkhuṁ anupakhajja seyyaṁ kappeyya “yassa sambādho bhavissati, so pakkamissatī”ti etadeva paccayaṁ karitvā anaññaṁ, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If, in a dwelling belonging to the Sangha, a monk arranges his sleeping place in a way that encroaches on a monk that he knows arrived there before him, with the intention that anyone who feels crowded will leave, and he does so only for this reason and no other, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If, in a dwelling belonging to the Sangha, a monk arranges his sleeping place in a way that encroaches on a monk that he knows arrived there before him, with the intention that anyone who feels crowded will leave, and he does so only for this reason and no other, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 17: evicting a monk

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 17: Throwing Out a Monastic

segments.pali: Yo pana bhikkhu bhikkhuṁ kupito anattamano saṅghikā vihārā nikkaḍḍheyya vā nikkaḍḍhāpeyya vā, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk, in anger, throws a monk out of a dwelling belonging to the Sangha, or has him thrown out, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk, in anger, throws a monk out of a dwelling belonging to the Sangha, or has him thrown out, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 18: sitting down on an upper story

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 18: The Upper Story of a Hut

segments.pali: Yo pana bhikkhu saṅghike vihāre uparivehāsakuṭiyā āhaccapādakaṁ mañcaṁ vā pīṭhaṁ vā abhinisīdeyya vā abhinipajjeyya vā, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk sits down or lies down on a bed or a bench with detachable legs on an upper story in a dwelling belonging to the Sangha, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk sits down or lies down on a bed or a bench with detachable legs on an upper story in a dwelling belonging to the Sangha, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 19: applying excessive materials to a large dwelling

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 19: Applying Materials to a Large Dwelling

segments.pali: Mahallakaṁ pana bhikkhunā vihāraṁ kārayamānena yāva dvārakosā aggaḷaṭṭhapanāya ālokasandhiparikammāya dvatticchadanassa pariyāyaṁ appaharite ṭhitena adhiṭṭhātabbaṁ, tato ce uttari appaharitepi ṭhito adhiṭṭhaheyya, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When a monk is building a large dwelling, then standing where there are no cultivated plants, he may apply two or three layers of roofing material, taking it as far as the doorcase and using it for fixing the door and for treating the window openings. If he applies more than that, even if he stands where there are no cultivated plants, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: ‘When a monk is building a large dwelling, then standing where there are no cultivated plants, he may apply two or three courses of roofing material, taking it as far as the doorcase and using it for fixing the door and for treating the window openings. If he applies more than that, even if he stands where there are no cultivated plants, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 20: pouring water with living beings in it

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 20: Using Water With Living Creatures

segments.pali: Yo pana bhikkhu jānaṁ sappāṇakaṁ udakaṁ tiṇaṁ vā mattikaṁ vā siñceyya vā siñcāpeyya vā, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk pours water that he knows contains living beings onto grass or clay, or has it poured, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk pours water that he knows contains living beings onto grass or clay, or has it poured, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: The second sub-chapter on vegetation is finished.

english.text: The subchapter on the instruction

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 21: instructing nuns without approval

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 21: Instruction

segments.pali: Yo pana bhikkhu asammato bhikkhuniyo ovadeyya, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk who has not been appointed instructs the nuns, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk who has not been appointed instructs the nuns, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 22: instructing nuns after sunset

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 22: Instructing Nuns After Sunset

segments.pali: Sammatopi ce bhikkhu atthaṅgate sūriye bhikkhuniyo ovadeyya, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Even if he has been appointed, if a monk instructs the nuns after sunset, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Even if he has been appointed, if a monk instructs the nuns after sunset, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 23: instructing nuns in a nunnery

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 23: Instructing Nuns in a Nunnery

segments.pali: Yo pana bhikkhu bhikkhunupassayaṁ upasaṅkamitvā bhikkhuniyo ovadeyya aññatra samayā, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk goes to a nunnery and instructs the nuns, except on an appropriate occasion, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk goes to the nuns’ dwelling place and then instructs them, except on an appropriate occasion, he commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Tatthāyaṁ samayo, gilānā hoti bhikkhunī, ayaṁ tattha samayo.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This is the appropriate occasion: a nun is sick.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: This is the appropriate occasion: a nun is sick.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 24: accusing monks of teaching nuns for profit

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 24: Teaching Nuns for Profit

segments.pali: Yo pana bhikkhu evaṁ vadeyya “āmisahetu therā bhikkhū bhikkhuniyo ovadantī”ti, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk says that the senior monks are instructing the nuns for the sake of worldly gain, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk says that the senior monks are instructing the nuns for the sake of worldly gain, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 25: giving robe-cloth to a nun

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 25: Giving Robes

segments.pali: Yo pana bhikkhu aññātikāya bhikkhuniyā cīvaraṁ dadeyya aññatra pārivattakā, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk gives robe-cloth to an unrelated nun, except in exchange, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk gives robe-cloth to an unrelated nun, except in exchange, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 26: sewing a robe for a nun

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 26: Sewing Robes

segments.pali: Yo pana bhikkhu aññātikāya bhikkhuniyā cīvaraṁ sibbeyya vā sibbāpeyya vā, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk sews a robe for an unrelated nun, or has one sewn, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk sews a robe for an unrelated nun, or has one sewn, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 27: traveling with a nun

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 27: Travelling by Arrangement

segments.pali: Yo pana bhikkhu bhikkhuniyā saddhiṁ saṁvidhāya ekaddhānamaggaṁ paṭipajjeyya antamaso gāmantarampi aññatra samayā, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk travels by arrangement with a nun, even just to the next inhabited area, except on an appropriate occasion, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk travels by appointment with a nun, even just to the next village, except on an appropriate occasion, he commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Tatthāyaṁ samayo, satthagamanīyo hoti maggo, sāsaṅkasammato, sappaṭibhayo, ayaṁ tattha samayo.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This is the appropriate occasion: the road is considered risky and dangerous and should be traveled with a group.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: This is the appropriate occasion: the road is considered risky and dangerous and should be traveled with a group.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 28: traveling on a boat with a nun

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 28: Travelling on a Boat With a Nun

segments.pali: Yo pana bhikkhu bhikkhuniyā saddhiṁ saṁvidhāya ekaṁ nāvaṁ abhiruheyya uddhaṅgāminiṁ vā adhogāminiṁ vā aññatra tiriyaṁ taraṇāya, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk boards a boat by arrangement with a nun, either to go upstream or downstream, except for the purpose of crossing, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk boards a boat by appointment with a nun, either to go upstream or downstream, except for the purpose of crossing, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 29: eating food prepared on a nun’s instruction

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 29: Eating Food Prepared on a Nun’s Instruction

segments.pali: Yo pana bhikkhu jānaṁ bhikkhuniparipācitaṁ piṇḍapātaṁ bhuñjeyya aññatra pubbe gihisamārambhā, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk eats almsfood knowing that a nun had it prepared, except if the householder had intended to prepare it anyway, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk eats almsfood knowing that a nun had it prepared, except if the householder had intended to prepare it anyway, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 30: sitting alone with a nun

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 30: Sitting in Private

segments.pali: Yo pana bhikkhu bhikkhuniyā saddhiṁ eko ekāya raho nisajjaṁ kappeyya, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk sits down in private alone with a nun, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk sits in private alone with a nun, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: The third sub-chapter on teaching is finished
