english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 31: eating excessively at a public guesthouse

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 31: Eating at a Public Guest-House

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk who is not sick eats more than one almsmeal at a public guesthouse,

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk who is not sick eats more than one alms-meal at a public guesthouse,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 32: eating in a group

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 32: Eating in a Group

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk eats in a group, except on an appropriate occasion, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk eats in a group, except on an appropriate occasion, he commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Tatthāyaṁ samayo, gilānasamayo, cīvaradānasamayo, cīvarakārasamayo, addhānagamanasamayo, nāvābhiruhanasamayo, mahāsamayo, samaṇabhattasamayo, ayaṁ tattha samayo.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: These are the appropriate occasions: he is sick; it is the robe-giving season; it is a time of making robes; he is traveling; he is on a boat; it is a big occasion; it is a meal given by a monastic.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: These are the appropriate occasions: he is sick; it is the robe-giving season; it is a time of making robes; he is traveling; he is on a boat; it is a big occasion; it is a meal given by a monastic.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 33: eating meals in succession

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 33: Eating Successive Meals

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk eats a meal before another, except on an appropriate occasion, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk eats a meal before another, except on an appropriate occasion, he commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Tatthāyaṁ samayo, gilānasamayo, cīvaradānasamayo, cīvarakārasamayo, ayaṁ tattha samayo.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: These are the appropriate occasions: he is sick; it is the robe-giving season; it is a time of making robes.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: These are the appropriate occasions: he is sick; it is the robe-giving season; it is a time of making robes.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 34: accepting more than three bowlfuls

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 34: Accepting Two or Three Bowls Full

segments.pali: Bhikkhuṁ paneva kulaṁ upagataṁ pūvehi vā manthehi vā abhihaṭṭhuṁ pavāreyya, ākaṅkhamānena bhikkhunā dvattipattapūrā paṭiggahetabbā. Tato ce uttari paṭiggaṇheyya, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk goes to a family and is invited to take cookies or crackers, he may accept two or three bowlfuls if he wishes. If he accepts more than that, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk goes to a family and is invited to take pastries or baked goods, he may accept two or three bowlfuls if he wishes. If he accepts more than that, he commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Dvattipattapūre paṭiggahetvā tato nīharitvā bhikkhūhi saddhiṁ saṁvibhajitabbaṁ, ayaṁ tattha sāmīci.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If he accepts two or three bowlfuls, he should take it away and share it with the monks. This is the proper procedure.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If he accepts two or three bowlfuls, he should take it away and share it with the monks. This is the proper procedure.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 35: eating more after refusing an invitation

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 35: Eating More After Refusing an Invitation

segments.pali: Yo pana bhikkhu bhuttāvī pavārito anatirittaṁ khādanīyaṁ vā bhojanīyaṁ vā khādeyya vā bhuñjeyya vā, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk has finished his meal and refused an invitation to eat more, and then eats fresh or cooked food that is not left over, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk has finished his meal and refused an invitation to eat more, and then eats fresh or cooked food that is not left over, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 36: encouraging a monastic to eat after refusing an invitation

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 36: Encouraging Another Monastic to Eat More After Refusing an Invitation

segments.pali: Yo pana bhikkhu bhikkhuṁ bhuttāviṁ pavāritaṁ anatirittena khādanīyena vā bhojanīyena vā abhihaṭṭhuṁ pavāreyya “handa bhikkhu khāda vā bhuñja vā”ti jānaṁ āsādanāpekkho, bhuttasmiṁ pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk invites a monk, whom he knows has finished his meal and refused an invitation to eat more, to eat fresh or cooked food that is not left over, saying, ‘Here, monk, eat,’ aiming to criticize him, then when the other has eaten, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk invites a monk, who he knows has finished his meal and refused an invitation to eat more, to eat fresh or cooked food that is not left over, saying, ‘Here, monk, eat,’ aiming to criticize him, then when the other has eaten, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 37: eating at the wrong time

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession  37: Eating at the Wrong Time

segments.pali: Yo pana bhikkhu vikāle khādanīyaṁ vā bhojanīyaṁ vā khādeyya vā bhuñjeyya vā, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk eats fresh or cooked food at the wrong time, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk eats fresh or cooked food at the wrong time, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 38: eating stored food

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 38: Storing Food

segments.pali: Yo pana bhikkhu sannidhikārakaṁ khādanīyaṁ vā bhojanīyaṁ vā khādeyya vā bhuñjeyya vā, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk eats fresh or cooked food that he has stored, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk eats fresh or cooked food that he has stored, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 39: asking for fine foods

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 39 Asking for Fine Foods

segments.pali: Yāni kho pana tāni paṇītabhojanāni, seyyathidaṁ—sappi, navanītaṁ, telaṁ, madhu, phāṇitaṁ, maccho, maṁsaṁ, khīraṁ, dadhi. Yo pana bhikkhu evarūpāni paṇītabhojanāni agilāno attano atthāya viññāpetvā bhuñjeyya, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk who is not sick asks for any of these kinds of fine foods for himself—that is, ghee, butter, oil, honey, syrup, fish, meat, milk, and curd—and then eats it, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk who is not sick asks for any of these kinds of fine foods for himself—that is, ghee, butter, oil, honey, syrup, fish, meat, milk, and curd—and then eats it, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 40: eating ungiven food

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 40: Eating Unoffered Food

segments.pali: Yo pana bhikkhu adinnaṁ mukhadvāraṁ āhāraṁ āhareyya aññatra udakadantaponā, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk eats food that has not been given, except for water and tooth cleaners, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk eats food that has not been given, except for water and tooth cleaners, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The fourth subchapter on eating is finished.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: The fourth sub-chapter on eating is finished

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The subchapter on naked ascetics

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 41: giving food to non-Buddhist monastics

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 41: Giving Food to Ascetics

segments.pali: Yo pana bhikkhu acelakassa vā paribbājakassa vā paribbājikāya vā sahatthā khādanīyaṁ vā bhojanīyaṁ vā dadeyya, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk personally gives fresh or cooked food to a naked ascetic, to a male wanderer, or to a female wanderer, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk gives fresh or cooked food to a naked ascetic, to a male wanderer, or to a female wanderer with his own hands, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 42: dismissing a monk during almsround

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 42: Sending a Monastic Back from Almsround

segments.pali: Yo pana bhikkhu bhikkhuṁ “ehāvuso, gāmaṁ vā nigamaṁ vā piṇḍāya pavisissāmā”ti tassa dāpetvā vā adāpetvā vā uyyojeyya “gacchāvuso, na me tayā saddhiṁ kathā vā nisajjā vā phāsu hoti, ekakassa me kathā vā nisajjā vā phāsu hotī”ti etadeva paccayaṁ karitvā anaññaṁ, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk says to a monk, ‘Come, let’s go to the village or town for alms,’ and then, whether he has had food given to him or not, sends him away, saying, ‘Go away! I’m not comfortable talking or sitting with you, but only if I talk and sit by myself,’ and he does so only for this reason and no other, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk says to a monk, ‘Come, let’s go to the village or town for alms,’ and then, whether he has had food given to him or not, sends him away, saying, ‘Go away, I’m not comfortable talking or sitting with you, but only if I talk and sit by myself,’ and he does so only for this reason and no other, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 43: intruding on a lustful couple

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 43: Intruding on a Couple Enjoying Themselves

segments.pali: Yo pana bhikkhu sabhojane kule anupakhajja nisajjaṁ kappeyya, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk sits down intruding on a lustful couple, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk sits down intruding on a lustful couple, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 44: sitting with a woman in a concealed place

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 44: Sitting in a Concealed Place

segments.pali: Yo pana bhikkhu mātugāmena saddhiṁ raho paṭicchanne āsane nisajjaṁ kappeyya, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk sits down in private on a concealed seat with a woman, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk sits down in private on a concealed seat with a woman, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 45: sitting alone with a woman

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 45: Sitting in Private

segments.pali: Yo pana bhikkhu mātugāmena saddhiṁ eko ekāya raho nisajjaṁ kappeyya, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk sits down in private alone with a woman, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk sits down in private alone with a woman, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 46: visiting families before a meal

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 46: Visiting Families Before a Meal

segments.pali: Yo pana bhikkhu nimantito sabhatto samāno santaṁ bhikkhuṁ anāpucchā purebhattaṁ vā pacchābhattaṁ vā kulesu cārittaṁ āpajjeyya aññatra samayā, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk who has been invited to a meal visits families beforehand or afterwards without informing an available monk, except on an appropriate occasion, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk who has been invited to a meal visits families beforehand or afterwards without informing an available monk, except on an appropriate occasion, he commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Tatthāyaṁ samayo, cīvaradānasamayo, cīvarakārasamayo, ayaṁ tattha samayo.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: These are the appropriate occasions: it is the robe-giving season; it is a time of making robes.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: These are the appropriate occasions: it is the robe-giving season; it is a time of making robes.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 47: accepting an invitation of more than four months

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 47: An Invitation for Four Months

segments.pali: Agilānena bhikkhunā catumāsappaccayapavāraṇā sāditabbā aññatra punapavāraṇāya, aññatra niccapavāraṇāya. Tato ce uttari sādiyeyya, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A monk who is not sick may accept an invitation to ask for requisites for four months. If he accepts one beyond that limit, except if it is a further invitation or a permanent invitation, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: A monk who is not sick may accept an invitation to ask for requisites for four months. If he accepts one beyond that limit, except if it is a further invitation or a permanent invitation, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 48: going to see a mobilized army

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 48: Seeing a Mobilizing Army

segments.pali: Yo pana bhikkhu uyyuttaṁ senaṁ dassanāya gaccheyya aññatra tathārūpappaccayā, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk goes to see an army, except if there is a suitable reason, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk goes to see an army, except if there is a suitable reason, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 49: staying more than three days with an army

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 49: Staying with an Army

segments.pali: Siyā ca tassa bhikkhuno kocideva paccayo senaṁ gamanāya, dirattatirattaṁ tena bhikkhunā senāya vasitabbaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If that monk has a reason for going to the army, he may stay with the army for two or three nights.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If that monk has a reason for going to the army, he may stay with the army for two or three nights.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If he stays longer than that, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If he stays longer than that, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 50: seeing an army in action

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 50. Viewing a Military Action

segments.pali: Dirattatirattaṁ ce bhikkhu senāya vasamāno uyyodhikaṁ vā balaggaṁ vā senābyūhaṁ vā anīkadassanaṁ vā gaccheyya, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk who is staying with an army for two or three nights goes to a battle, a troop review, a massing of the army, or a troop inspection, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk who is staying with an army for two or three nights goes to a battle, a troop review, a massing of the army, or a troop inspection, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The fifth subchapter on naked ascetics is finished.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: The fifth sub-chapter on naked ascetics is finished

english.author: pasanna

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk drinks this or that kind of alcoholic drink, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk tickles someone, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk plays in water, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk plays in water, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 54: Disrespect

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk is disrespectful, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk is disrespectful, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 55: scaring a monk

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 55: Scaring

segments.pali: Yo pana bhikkhu bhikkhuṁ bhiṁsāpeyya, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk scares a monk, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk scares a monk, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 56: lighting a fire

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 56: Lighting a Fire

segments.pali: Yo pana bhikkhu agilāno visibbanāpekkho jotiṁ samādaheyya vā samādahāpeyya vā aññatra tathārūpappaccayā, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk who is not sick lights a fire to warm himself, or has one lit, except if there is a suitable reason, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk who is not sick lights a fire to warm himself, or has one lit, except if there is a suitable reason, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 57: bathing too often

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 57: Bathing

segments.pali: Yo pana bhikkhu orenaddhamāsaṁ nahāyeyya aññatra samayā, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk bathes at intervals of less than a half-month, except on an appropriate occasion, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk bathes at intervals of less than a half-month, except on an appropriate occasion, he commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Tatthāyaṁ samayo “diyaḍḍho māso seso gimhāna”nti “vassānassa paṭhamo māso” iccete aḍḍhateyyamāsā uṇhasamayo, pariḷāhasamayo, gilānasamayo, kammasamayo, addhānagamanasamayo, vātavuṭṭhisamayo, ayaṁ tattha samayo.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: These are the appropriate occasions: it is the two-and-a-half-month period of summer and the fever season, comprising the last one-and-a-half months of summer and the first month of the rainy season; he is sick; he is working; he is traveling; there is wind and rain.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: These are the appropriate occasions: it is the two-and-a-half-month period of the hot season and the fever season, comprising the last one-and-a-half months of summer and the first month of the rainy season; he is sick; he is working; he is traveling; there is wind and rain.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 58: not making stains on a new robe

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 58: Making Stains

segments.pali: Navaṁ pana bhikkhunā cīvaralābhena tiṇṇaṁ dubbaṇṇakaraṇānaṁ aññataraṁ dubbaṇṇakaraṇaṁ ādātabbaṁ nīlaṁ vā kaddamaṁ vā kāḷasāmaṁ vā. Anādā ce bhikkhu tiṇṇaṁ dubbaṇṇakaraṇānaṁ aññataraṁ dubbaṇṇakaraṇaṁ navaṁ cīvaraṁ paribhuñjeyya, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When a monk gets a new robe, he should apply one of three kinds of stains: blue-green, mud color, or dark brown. If a monk uses a new robe without applying any of the three kinds of stains, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: When a monk gets a new robe, he should apply one of three kinds of stains: blue-green, mud-color, or dark brown. If a monk uses a new robe without applying any of the three kinds of stains, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 59: using assigned property that has not been relinquished

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 59: Transferring to Another

segments.pali: Yo pana bhikkhu bhikkhussa vā bhikkhuniyā vā sikkhamānāya vā sāmaṇerassa vā sāmaṇeriyā vā sāmaṁ cīvaraṁ vikappetvā appaccuddhāraṇaṁ paribhuñjeyya, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk himself assigns the ownership of a robe to a monk, a nun, a trainee nun, a novice monk, or a novice nun, and he then uses it without the other first relinquishing it, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk himself assigns the ownership of a robe to a monk, to a nun, to a trainee nun, to a novice monk, or to a novice nun, and he then uses it without the other first relinquishing it, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 60: hiding a robe

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 60: Hiding Robes

segments.pali: Yo pana bhikkhu bhikkhussa pattaṁ vā cīvaraṁ vā nisīdanaṁ vā sūcigharaṁ vā kāyabandhanaṁ vā apanidheyya vā apanidhāpeyya vā antamaso hasāpekkhopi, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk hides a monk’s bowl, robe, sitting mat, needle case, or belt, or he has it hidden, even just for a laugh, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk hides a monk’s bowl, robe, sitting mat, needle case, or belt, or he has it hidden, even just for a laugh, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The sixth subchapter on drinking alcohol is finished.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: The sixth sub-chapter on drinking alcohol if finished

english.text: The subchapter on containing living beings

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 61: intentionally killing a living being

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 61: Intentionally Killing Living Creatures

segments.pali: Yo pana bhikkhu sañcicca pāṇaṁ jīvitā voropeyya, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk intentionally kills a living being, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk intentionally kills a living being, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 62: using water with living beings in it

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 62: Using Water with Living Creatures

segments.pali: Yo pana bhikkhu jānaṁ sappāṇakaṁ udakaṁ paribhuñjeyya, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk uses water that he knows contains living beings, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk uses water that he knows contains living beings, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 63: reopening a settled legal issue

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 63: Reopening a Legal Issue

segments.pali: Yo pana bhikkhu jānaṁ yathādhammaṁ nihatādhikaraṇaṁ punakammāya ukkoṭeyya, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk reopens a legal issue that he knows has been legitimately settled, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk reopens a legal issue that he knows has been legitimately settled, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 64: concealing a grave offense

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 64: Concealing a Grave Offense

segments.pali: Yo pana bhikkhu bhikkhussa jānaṁ duṭṭhullaṁ āpattiṁ paṭicchādeyya, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk knowingly conceals a monk’s grave offense, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk knowingly conceals a monk’s grave offense, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 65: ordaining a person under twenty

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 65: Ordaining Under Twenty

segments.pali: Yo pana bhikkhu jānaṁ ūnavīsativassaṁ puggalaṁ upasampādeyya, so ca puggalo anupasampanno, te ca bhikkhū gārayhā, idaṁ tasmiṁ pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk gives the full ordination to a person he knows is less than twenty years old, he commits an offense entailing confession. Moreover, that person has not received the full ordination and those monks are blameworthy.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk gives the full ordination to a person he knows is less than twenty years old, he commits an offense entailing confession. Moreover, that person has not received the full ordination and those monks are blameworthy.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 66: traveling with thieves

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 66: Travelling With Thieves

segments.pali: Yo pana bhikkhu jānaṁ theyyasatthena saddhiṁ saṁvidhāya ekaddhānamaggaṁ paṭipajjeyya antamaso gāmantarampi, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk knowingly travels by arrangement with a group of thieves, even just to the next inhabited area, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk knowingly travels by appointment with a group of thieves, even just to the next village, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 67: traveling with a woman

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 67 Travelling by Arrangement

segments.pali: Yo pana bhikkhu mātugāmena saddhiṁ saṁvidhāya ekaddhānamaggaṁ paṭipajjeyya antamaso gāmantarampi, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk travels by arrangement with a woman, even just to the next inhabited area, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk travels by appointment with a woman, even just to the next village, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 68: not giving up a bad view

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 68: Obstructions

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk says,

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk says,

segments.pali: “tathāhaṁ bhagavatā dhammaṁ desitaṁ ājānāmi, yathā yeme antarāyikā dhammā vuttā bhagavatā, te paṭisevato nālaṁ antarāyāyā”ti,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘As I understand the Buddha’s Teaching, the things he calls obstacles are unable to obstruct one who indulges in them,’

english.author: pasanna
english.text: ‘As I understand the Buddha’s Teaching, the things he calls obstructive are unable to obstruct one who indulges in them,’

english.author: brahmali
english.text: then the monks should correct him like this:

english.author: pasanna
english.text: then the monks should correct him like this:

segments.pali: “māyasmā evaṁ avaca, mā bhagavantaṁ abbhācikkhi, na hi sādhu bhagavato abbhakkhānaṁ, na hi bhagavā evaṁ vadeyya,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘No, venerable, don’t misrepresent the Buddha, for it’s not good to misrepresent the Buddha. The Buddha would never say such a thing.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: ‘No, Venerable, don’t misrepresent the Buddha, for it’s not good to misrepresent the Buddha. The Buddha would never say such a thing.

segments.pali: anekapariyāyenāvuso antarāyikā dhammā antarāyikā vuttā bhagavatā, alañca pana te paṭisevato antarāyāyā”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: In many discourses the Buddha has declared the obstacles to be obstructive and how they obstruct one who indulges in them.’

english.author: pasanna
english.text: In many discourses the Buddha has declared the obstructive things to be obstructive and how they obstruct one who indulges in them.’

segments.pali: Evañca so bhikkhu bhikkhūhi vuccamāno tatheva paggaṇheyya, so bhikkhu bhikkhūhi yāvatatiyaṁ samanubhāsitabbo tassa paṭinissaggāya.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If that monk continues as before, the monks should press him up to three times to make him give up that view.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If that monk continues as before, the monks should press him up to three times to make him stop.

segments.pali: Yāvatatiyañce samanubhāsiyamāno taṁ paṭinissajjeyya, iccetaṁ kusalaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If he then gives it up, all is well.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If he then stops, all is well.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If he does not, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 69: living with an ejected monk

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 69: Living With an Ejected Monastic

segments.pali: Yo pana bhikkhu jānaṁ tathāvādinā bhikkhunā akaṭānudhammena taṁ diṭṭhiṁ appaṭinissaṭṭhena saddhiṁ sambhuñjeyya vā, saṁvaseyya vā, saha vā seyyaṁ kappeyya, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk lives, does formal meetings, or shares a sleeping place with a monk who he knows is saying such things, who has not made amends according to the rule, and who has not given up that view, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk lives, does formal meetings, or shares a sleeping place with a monk who he knows is saying such things, who has not made amends according to the rule, and who has not given up that view, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna

english.text: Also if a novice monastic says,

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Also if a novice monastic says,

english.text: ‘As I understand the Buddha’s Teaching, the things he calls obstructive are unable to obstruct one who indulges in them,’

english.text: then the monks should correct him like this:

english.author: pasanna
english.text: then the monks should correct him like this:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘No, don’t misrepresent the Buddha, for it’s not good to misrepresent the Buddha. The Buddha would never say such a thing.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: ‘No, don’t misrepresent the Buddha, for it’s not good to misrepresent the Buddha. The Buddha would never say such a thing.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: In many discourses the Buddha has declared the obstacles to be obstructive and how they obstruct one who indulges in them.’

english.author: pasanna
english.text: In many discourses the Buddha has declared the obstructive things to be obstructive and how they obstruct one who indulges in them.’

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If that novice monastic continues as before, he should be told:

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If that novice monastic continues as before, he should be told:

segments.pali: “ajjatagge te, āvuso, samaṇuddesa na ceva so bhagavā satthā apadisitabbo,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘From today on you may not refer to the Buddha as your teacher.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: ‘From today on you may not refer to the Buddha as your teacher.

segments.pali: yampi caññe samaṇuddesā labhanti bhikkhūhi saddhiṁ dirattatirattaṁ sahaseyyaṁ, sāpi te natthi,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: And, unlike other novices, you can no longer share a sleeping place with the monks for two or three nights.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: And you can no longer share a sleeping place with the monks for two or three nights, as can other novice monastics.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Go! Away with you!’

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Go! Away with you!’

segments.pali: Yo pana bhikkhu jānaṁ tathānāsitaṁ samaṇuddesaṁ upalāpeyya vā, upaṭṭhāpeyya vā, sambhuñjeyya vā, saha vā seyyaṁ kappeyya, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk befriends that novice monastic, or he is attended on by him, lives with him, or shares a sleeping place with him, even though he knows that he has been expelled in this way, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk supports that novice monastic, or he is attended on by him, lives with him, or shares a sleeping place with him, even though he knows that he has been expelled in this way, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The seventh subchapter on containing living beings is finished.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: The seventh sub-chapter on containing living beings is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The subchapter on legitimate correction

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 71: not accepting legitimate correction

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 71: Legitimate Correction

segments.pali: Yo pana bhikkhu bhikkhūhi sahadhammikaṁ vuccamāno evaṁ vadeyya “na tāvāhaṁ, āvuso, etasmiṁ sikkhāpade sikkhissāmi, yāva na aññaṁ bhikkhuṁ byattaṁ vinayadharaṁ paripucchāmī”ti, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk, when legitimately corrected by the monks, says, ‘I won’t practice this training rule until I’ve questioned a monk who is an expert on the Monastic Law,’ he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk, when legitimately corrected by the monks, says, I won’t practice this training rule until I’ve questioned a monk who is an expert on the Monastic Law,’ he commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Sikkhamānena, bhikkhave, bhikkhunā aññātabbaṁ paripucchitabbaṁ paripañhitabbaṁ, ayaṁ tattha sāmīci.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A monk who is training should understand, should question, should enquire. This is the proper procedure.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: A monk who is training should understand, should question, should enquire. This is the proper procedure.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 72: disparaging the training rules

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 72: Annoyance

segments.pali: Yo pana bhikkhu pātimokkhe uddissamāne evaṁ vadeyya “kiṁ panimehi khuddānukhuddakehi sikkhāpadehi uddiṭṭhehi, yāvadeva kukkuccāya vihesāya vilekhāya saṁvattantī”ti, sikkhāpadavivaṇṇake pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When the Monastic Code is being recited, if a monk says, ‘What’s the point of reciting these minor training rules, when they just lead to anxiety, oppression, and annoyance?’ then in disparaging the training rules, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: When the Monastic Code is being recited, if a monk says, ‘What’s the point of reciting these minor and insignificant training rules, when they just lead to anxiety, oppression, and annoyance?’ then in disparaging the training rules, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 73: deception

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 73: Deception

segments.pali: Yo pana bhikkhu anvaddhamāsaṁ pātimokkhe uddissamāne evaṁ vadeyya

english.author: brahmali
english.text: During the half-monthly recitation of the Monastic Code, a monk might say,

english.author: pasanna
english.text: During the half-monthly recitation of the Monastic Code, a monk might say,

segments.pali: “idāneva kho ahaṁ jānāmi, ayampi kira dhammo suttāgato suttapariyāpanno anvaddhamāsaṁ uddesaṁ āgacchatī”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘Just now did I find out that this rule too has come down in the Monastic Code, is included in the Monastic Code, and comes up for recitation every half-month.’

english.author: pasanna
english.text: ‘Just now did I find out that this rule too has come down in the Monastic Code, is included in the Monastic Code, and comes up for recitation every half-month.’

segments.pali: Tañce bhikkhuṁ aññe bhikkhū jāneyyuṁ nisinnapubbaṁ iminā bhikkhunā dvattikkhattuṁ pātimokkhe uddissamāne, ko pana vādo bhiyyo, na ca tassa bhikkhuno aññāṇakena mutti atthi, yañca tattha āpattiṁ āpanno, tañca yathādhammo kāretabbo, uttari cassa moho āropetabbo

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If other monks know that that monk has previously sat through at least two or three recitations of the Monastic Code, then that monk is not let off because of ignorance, and he is to be dealt with according to the rule. Further, he should be charged with deception:

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If other monks know that that monk has previously sat through at least two or three recitations of the Monastic Code, then that monk is not let off because of ignorance, and he is to be dealt with according to the rule. Further, he should be charged with deception:

segments.pali: “tassa te, āvuso, alābhā, tassa te dulladdhaṁ, yaṁ tvaṁ pātimokkhe uddissamānena sādhukaṁ aṭṭhiṁ katvā manasi karosī”ti,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘It’s a loss for you that you don’t pay proper attention during the recitation of the Monastic Code.’

english.author: pasanna
english.text: It’s a loss for you that you don’t pay proper attention during the recitation of the Monastic Code.’

english.author: brahmali
english.text: And for the act of deception, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: And for the act of deception, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 74: hitting in anger

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 74: Hitting

segments.pali: Yo pana bhikkhu bhikkhussa kupito anattamano pahāraṁ dadeyya, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk hits a monk in anger, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk hits a monk in anger, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 75: raising a hand in anger

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 75: Raising a Hand

segments.pali: Yo pana bhikkhu bhikkhussa kupito anattamano talasattikaṁ uggireyya, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk raises a hand in anger against a monk, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk raises a hand in anger against a monk, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 76: making a groundless accusation

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 76: Groundless Accusation

segments.pali: Yo pana bhikkhu bhikkhuṁ amūlakena saṅghādisesena anuddhaṁseyya, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk groundlessly charges a monk with an offense entailing suspension, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk groundlessly charges a monk with an offense entailing suspension, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 77: intentionally making a monk anxious

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 77: Intentionally Making a Monastic Anxious

segments.pali: Yo pana bhikkhu bhikkhussa sañcicca kukkuccaṁ upadaheyya “itissa muhuttampi aphāsu bhavissatī”ti etadeva paccayaṁ karitvā anaññaṁ, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk intentionally makes a monk anxious, thinking, ‘In this way he will be ill at ease at least for a moment,’ and he does so only for this reason and no other, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk intentionally makes a monk anxious, thinking, In this way he will be ill at ease at least for a moment,’ and he does so only for this reason and no other, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 78: eavesdropping

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 78: Eavesdropping

segments.pali: Yo pana bhikkhu bhikkhūnaṁ bhaṇḍanajātānaṁ kalahajātānaṁ vivādāpannānaṁ upassutiṁ tiṭṭheyya “yaṁ ime bhaṇissanti, taṁ sossāmī”ti etadeva paccayaṁ karitvā anaññaṁ, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk eavesdrops on monks who are arguing and disputing, thinking, ‘I’ll hear what they say,’ and he does so only for this reason and no other, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk eavesdrops on monks who are arguing and disputing, thinking, ‘I’ll hear what they say,’ and he does so only for this reason and no other, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 79: criticizing a legitimate legal procedure

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 79: Obstructing a Legal Procedure

segments.pali: Yo pana bhikkhu dhammikānaṁ kammānaṁ chandaṁ datvā pacchā khīyanadhammaṁ āpajjeyya, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk gives his consent to legitimate legal procedures, and then criticizes them afterwards, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk gives his consent to legitimate legal procedures, and then criticizes them afterwards, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 80: leaving without giving consent

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 80: Leaving Without Giving Consent

segments.pali: Yo pana bhikkhu saṅghe vinicchayakathāya vattamānāya chandaṁ adatvā uṭṭhāyāsanā pakkameyya, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When the Sangha is in the middle of a discussion, if a monk gets up from his seat and leaves without first giving his consent, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: When the Sangha is in the middle of a discussion, if a monk gets up from his seat and leaves without first giving his consent, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 81: criticizing a robe distribution

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 81: Criticizing Robe Distribution

segments.pali: Yo pana bhikkhu samaggena saṅghena cīvaraṁ datvā pacchā khīyanadhammaṁ āpajjeyya “yathāsanthutaṁ bhikkhū saṅghikaṁ lābhaṁ pariṇāmentī”ti, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk gives out a robe as part of a unanimous Sangha and then criticizes it afterwards, saying, ‘The monks are diverting the Sangha’s material support according to friendship,’ he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk gives out a robe as part of a unanimous Sangha and then criticizes it afterwards, saying, ‘The monks are diverting the Sangha’s material gains according to friendship,’ he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 82: diverting gains intended for the Sangha

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 82: Diverting Gains

segments.pali: Yo pana bhikkhu jānaṁ saṅghikaṁ lābhaṁ pariṇataṁ puggalassa pariṇāmeyya, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk diverts to an individual material support that he knows was intended for the Sangha, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk diverts to an individual material support that he knows was intended for the Sangha, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The eighth subchapter on legitimate correction is finished.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: The eighth sub-chapter on legitimate correction is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The subchapter on precious things

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 83: entering a royal compound unannounced

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 83: Royal Compounds

segments.pali: Yo pana bhikkhu rañño khattiyassa muddhābhisittassa anikkhantarājake aniggataratanake pubbe appaṭisaṁvidito indakhīlaṁ atikkāmeyya, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk, without first being announced, crosses the threshold to the bedroom of a consecrated aristocrat king, when both the king and the queen are present, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk, without first being announced, crosses the threshold to the bedroom of a consecrated aristocrat king, when both the king and the queen are present, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 84: picking up precious things

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 84: Precious Things

segments.pali: Yo pana bhikkhu ratanaṁ vā ratanasammataṁ vā aññatra ajjhārāmā vā ajjhāvasathā vā uggaṇheyya vā uggaṇhāpeyya vā, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk picks up something precious or something regarded as precious, or he has it picked up, except within a monastery or inside a lodging, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk picks up something precious or something regarded as precious, or he has it picked up, except within a monastery or inside a lodging, he commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Ratanaṁ vā pana bhikkhunā ratanasammataṁ vā ajjhārāme vā ajjhāvasathe vā uggahetvā vā uggahāpetvā vā nikkhipitabbaṁ “yassa bhavissati, so harissatī”ti, ayaṁ tattha sāmīci.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If he picks up something precious or something regarded as precious, or he has it picked up, within a monastery or inside a lodging, he should put it aside with the thought, ‘Whoever owns it will come and get it.’ This is the proper procedure.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If he picks up something precious or something regarded as precious, or he has it picked up, within a monastery or inside a lodging, he should put it aside with the thought, ‘Whoever owns it will fetch it.’ This is the proper procedure.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 85: entering an inhabited area at the wrong time

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 85: Entering a Village at the Wrong Time

segments.pali: Yo pana bhikkhu santaṁ bhikkhuṁ anāpucchāvikāle gāmaṁ paviseyya aññatra tathārūpā accāyikā karaṇīyā, pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk, when another monk is available, enters an inhabited area at the wrong time without informing him, except if there is some appropriate urgent business, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk, when another monk is available, enters an inhabited area at the wrong time without informing him, except if there is some appropriate urgent business, he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 86: making an inappropriate needle case

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 86: Needle Case

segments.pali: Yo pana bhikkhu aṭṭhimayaṁ vā dantamayaṁ vā visāṇamayaṁ vā sūcigharaṁ kārāpeyya, bhedanakaṁ pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk has a needle case made from bone, ivory, or horn, it is to be destroyed, and he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk has a needle case made from bone, ivory, or horn, it is to be destroyed, and he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 87: making an inappropriate bed or bench

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 87: Beds and Benches

segments.pali: Navaṁ pana bhikkhunā mañcaṁ vā pīṭhaṁ vā kārayamānena aṭṭhaṅgulapādakaṁ kāretabbaṁ sugataṅgulena aññatra heṭṭhimāya aṭaniyā. Taṁ atikkāmayato chedanakaṁ pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk is having a new bed or bench made, it is to have legs eight standard fingerbreadths long below the lowest frame. If the legs exceed that, they are to be cut down, and he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk is having a new bed or bench made, it is to have legs eight standard fingerbreadths long below the lowest frame. If the legs exceed that, they are to be cut down, and he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 88: making an upholstered bed or bench

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 88: Upholstery

segments.pali: Yo pana bhikkhu mañcaṁ vā pīṭhaṁ vā tūlonaddhaṁ kārāpeyya, uddālanakaṁ pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk has a bed or a bench made upholstered with cotton down, it is to be stripped, and he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk has a bed or a bench made upholstered with cotton down, it is to be stripped, and he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 89: making an oversize sitting mat

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 89: Sitting Mats

segments.pali: Nisīdanaṁ pana bhikkhunā kārayamānena pamāṇikaṁ kāretabbaṁ, tatridaṁ pamāṇaṁ, dīghaso dve vidatthiyo sugatavidatthiyā, tiriyaṁ diyaḍḍhaṁ, dasā vidatthi. Taṁ atikkāmayato chedanakaṁ pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk is having a sitting mat made, it should be made the right size. This is the right size: two standard handspans long and one-and-a-half wide, and a border of one handspan. If it exceeds that, it is to be cut down, and he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk is having a sitting mat made, it should be made the right size. This is the right size: two standard handspans long and one-and-a-half wide, and a border of one handspan. If it exceeds that, it is to be cut down, and he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 90: making an oversize itch cloth

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 90: Itch Bandage

segments.pali: Kaṇḍuppaṭicchādiṁ pana bhikkhunā kārayamānena pamāṇikā kāretabbā, tatridaṁ pamāṇaṁ, dīghaso catasso vidatthiyo sugatavidatthiyā, tiriyaṁ dve vidatthiyo. Taṁ atikkāmayato chedanakaṁ pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk is having an itch-covering cloth made, it should be made the right size. This is the right size: four standard handspans long and two wide. If it exceeds that, it is to be cut down, and he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk is having an itch-covering cloth made, it should be made the right size. This is the right size: four standard handspans long and two wide. If it exceeds that, it is to be cut down, and he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 91: making an oversize rainy-season robe

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 91: Rainy-Season Robes

segments.pali: Vassikasāṭikaṁ pana bhikkhunā kārayamānena pamāṇikā kāretabbā, tatridaṁ pamāṇaṁ, dīghaso cha vidatthiyo sugatavidatthiyā, tiriyaṁ aḍḍhateyyā. Taṁ atikkāmayato chedanakaṁ pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk is having a rainy-season robe made, it should be made the right size. This is the right size: six standard handspans long and two-and-a-half wide. If it exceeds that, it is to be cut down, and he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk is having a rainy-season robe made, it should be made the right size. This is the right size: six standard handspans long and two-and-a-half wide. If it exceeds that, it is to be cut down, and he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Confession 92: making an oversize robe

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Confession 92: Robe Size

segments.pali: Yo pana bhikkhu sugatacīvarappamāṇaṁ cīvaraṁ kārāpeyya, atirekaṁ vā, chedanakaṁ pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk has a robe made that is the standard robe size or larger, it is to be cut down, and he commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk has a robe made that is the standard robe measure or larger, it is to be cut down, and he commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Tatridaṁ sugatassa sugatacīvarappamāṇaṁ, dīghaso nava vidatthiyo sugatavidatthiyā, tiriyaṁ cha vidatthiyo, idaṁ sugatassa sugatacīvarapamāṇanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This is the standard robe size: nine standard handspans long and six wide.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: This is the standard robe measure: nine standard handspans long and six wide.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The ninth subchapter on precious things is finished.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: The nineth sub-chapter on valuables is finished.

segments.pali: Uddiṭṭhā kho āyasmanto dvenavuti pācittiyā dhammā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Venerables, the ninety-two rules on confession have been recited.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Venerables, the ninety-two rules on confession have been recited.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: In regard to this I ask you, ‘Are you pure in this?’

english.author: pasanna
english.text: In regard to this I ask you, ‘Are you pure in this?’

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A second time I ask, ‘Are you pure in this?’

english.author: pasanna
english.text: A second time I ask, ‘Are you pure in this?’

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A third time I ask, ‘Are you pure in this?’

english.author: pasanna
english.text: A third time I ask, ‘Are you pure in this?’

english.author: brahmali
english.text: You are pure in this and therefore silent. I will remember it thus.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: You are pure in this and therefore silent. I will remember it thus.’

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The chapter on offenses entailing confession is finished.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: The Chapter on offenses entailing confession is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The rules on acknowledgement

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Recitation of the Rules of Acknowledgement

segments.pali: Ime kho panāyasmanto cattāro pāṭidesanīyā dhammā uddesaṁ āgacchanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Venerables, these four rules on acknowledgment come up for recitation.

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Venerables, these four rules on acknowledgment come up for recitation.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Acknowledgement 1: receiving a nun’s food

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Acknowledgement 1: Receiving a Nun’s Food

segments.pali: Yo pana bhikkhu aññātikāya bhikkhuniyā antaragharaṁ paviṭṭhāya hatthato khādanīyaṁ vā bhojanīyaṁ vā sahatthā paṭiggahetvā khādeyya vā bhuñjeyya vā, paṭidesetabbaṁ tena bhikkhunā

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a monk receives fresh or cooked food directly from an unrelated nun who has entered an inhabited area, and then eats it, he must acknowledge it:

english.author: pasanna
english.text: If a monk receives fresh or cooked food directly from an unrelated nun who has entered an inhabited area, and then eats it, he must acknowledge it:

segments.pali: “gārayhaṁ, āvuso, dhammaṁ āpajjiṁ asappāyaṁ pāṭidesanīyaṁ, taṁ paṭidesemī”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘I have done a blameworthy and unsuitable thing that is to be acknowledged. I acknowledge it.’

english.author: pasanna

english.author: pasanna
english.text: Acknowledgement 2: Letting a Nun Order Food

segments.pali: Bhikkhū paneva kulesu nimantitā bhuñjanti, tatra ce sā bhikkhunī vosāsamānarūpā ṭhitā hoti “idha sūpaṁ detha, idha odanaṁ dethā”ti. Tehi bhikkhūhi sā bhikkhunī apasādetabbā “apasakka tāva bhagini, yāva bhikkhū bhuñjantī”ti. Ekassapi ce bhikkhuno na paṭibhāseyya taṁ bhikkhuniṁ apasādetuṁ “apasakka tāva bhagini, yāva bhikkhū bhuñjantī”ti, paṭidesetabbaṁ tehi bhikkhūhi

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When monks eat by invitation to families, if a nun is there giving directions, saying, ‘Give bean curry here; give rice there,’ then those monks should stop her: ‘Stop, sister, while the monks are eating.’ If not even a single monk addresses that nun in this way to stop her, they must acknowledge it:

english.author: pasanna
english.text: When monks eat by invitation to families, if a nun is there giving directions, saying, “Give bean curry here; give rice there,” then those monks should stop her: “Stop, Sister, while the monks are eating.” If not even a single monk addresses that nun in this way to stop her, they must acknowledge it:

segments.pali: “gārayhaṁ, āvuso, dhammaṁ āpajjimhā asappāyaṁ pāṭidesanīyaṁ, taṁ paṭidesemā”ti.
